paper_title: Statistical Analysis of Pollen Stress Effects on Bee Worker Performance and Survival

methods.method_name: Statistical Analysis of Worker Performance and Survival
methods.description: This method describes the statistical procedures used to analyze the effects of colony-level pollen stress on worker performance (participation, weight) and survival. It involves comparing worker participation using chi-square tests, comparing treatment means for performance metrics using t-tests and ANOVAs with Bonferroni correction and Tukey-Kramer post-hoc tests, and comparing worker survival using Kaplan-Meier estimates and log-rank tests with Šidák adjustments.

steps.description: Numbers of focal workers that did or did not participate in a task were compared among treatments with 2x2 (trial 1) or 2x3 contingency tables (trials 2 and 3) using chi-square tests of independence.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform chi-square test of independence on contingency table to compare worker participation

steps.description: compare treatment means separately for each trial by t-test (trial 1) or ANOVA (trials 2 and 3)
steps.searchable_summary: Compare treatment means using t-test (Trial 1) or ANOVA (Trials 2, 3)

steps.description: applied a Bonferroni correction to α = 0.05 to account for conducting multiple tests.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply Bonferroni correction to the significance level

steps.description: Post-hoc pairwise comparisons were made using the Tukey-Kramer procedure where treatment effects were significant.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Tukey-Kramer post-hoc test for pairwise comparisons

steps.description: The survival of workers reared in pollen-limited and pollen-abundant treatment conditions was compared with Kaplan-Meier estimates of survival
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Kaplan-Meier survival estimates and curves
inputs: Worker survival data (time to event or censoring)

steps.description: post-hoc comparisons were made with Šidák adjustments to log-rank tests.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform log-rank test with Šidák adjustment for post-hoc survival comparisons
outputs: Pairwise comparison results for survival curves

steps.description: T-tests, ANOVAs, and survivorship analyses were conducted using SAS version 9.3 (SAS Institute, Inc., Cary, NC, U.S.A.); contingency tests were conducted with an open-access statistical calculator (physics.csbsju.edu/stats/contingency. html).
steps.searchable_summary: Specify software and tools used for statistical analysis

steps.description: The dataset is available as Supporting Information (S1 File).
steps.searchable_summary: Note dataset availability as supporting information
